title: Jacob Andreas and Brett McGuire named Edgerton Award winners
summary: The associate professors of EECS and chemistry, respectively, are honored for exceptional contributions to teaching, research, and service at MIT.
author: Danielle Randall Doughty | Jane Halpern | Department of Chemistry | Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science

title: Bringing AI-driven protein-design tools to biologists everywhere
summary: Founded by Tristan Bepler PhD ’20 and former MIT professor Tim Lu PhD ’07, OpenProtein.AI offers researchers open-source models and other tools for protein engineering.
author: Zach Winn | MIT News

title: Amazon and data collection
summary: Why it matters: Explore how Amazon collects user data through Alexa, Ring, AWS, and shopping habits. Learn risks, privacy laws, GDPR fines, and how to protect your data.
author: Sanksshep Mahendra

title: AI takes control of quantum computers
summary: NVIDIA has released Ising, its first open-source AI models built to solve quantum computing’s biggest bottlenecks: calibration and error correction. By acting as the control plane for quantum machines, Ising cuts calibration time from days to hours and delivers up to 2.5× faster, 3× more accurate error correction.

title: Why triage alerts - when AI can do it for you? by Brad Woodberg
summary: If you ask security analysts to describe the biggest pain points in their role, you will no doubt get a diverse set of answers. One thing that they will almost certainly have in common is the challenge of dealing with alert fatigue.
